_comment: A REMOTE MCP SERVER, doormanned. Every call is the SAME POST to the SAME path — method+path classification is completely blind here. The tool being invoked lives in the BODY (params.name), so the body selector IS the classifier. This adapter is the argument for body-aware classification: without it, MCP is unpoliceable.
_comment_annotations: MCP tools self-declare readOnlyHint / destructiveHint / idempotentHint. DO NOT classify from those. They are asserted by the very server you are constraining — a malicious server declaring readOnlyHint:true on exfiltrate_everything walks straight through. Use them to GENERATE a draft of this file for a human to review and sign. The vendor publishes the interface; it does not publish danger.

classify._: === HANDSHAKE / DISCOVERY — free, but not unclassified. ===

classify._note: Listing tools is harmless. Note the server may ADD tools at runtime (notifications/tools/list_changed) — any tool not classified below hits the default and is DENIED until a human classifies it. That property matters far more here than on a REST API: GitHub ships endpoints quarterly, an MCP server can ship one mid-conversation.
You cannot unsend it. Irreversible socially, not technically — a different axis from destructive, which is exactly why effects are a set.

classify._: === THE REVERSE CHANNEL — server → client, over SSE. ===
classify.__: MCP is bidirectional. The SERVER can send requests to the CLIENT: sampling/createMessage asks your agent's LLM to generate text and hands the result back to the server; elicitation/create renders a dialog to YOUR HUMAN. That is a data-exfiltration and model-hijack channel pointing the wrong way through the proxy, and no amount of request-side authorization touches it. It is classified here so it can be DENIED BY DEFAULT: no slip that omits these scopes lets a remote server drive your model or prompt your user.

classify._note: The server asks OUR model to generate. Almost no agent should ever grant this.
The server renders a dialog to OUR human — a consent prompt on a channel the vendor controls.
The server asks which filesystems we expose. Reconnaissance.
